channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: UK Social Media Ban for Minors Has Privacy Experts Worried
item.description: The UK will ban adolescents under 16 years old from user-to-user social-media platforms, despite age-verification issues and privacy concerns....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: 144 Mastra npm Packages Compromised via Hijacked Contributor Account
item.description: As many as 144 npm packages associated with the Mastra namespace ("@mastra/*"), a popular open-source JavaScript and TypeScript framework for building artificial intelligence (AI) applications, have b...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Kodak confirms data breach claimed by ShinyHunters extortion gang
item.description: Kodak has confirmed that it's working with external cybersecurity experts to investigate a security breach after hackers gained access to some of the company's data. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: CISA Warns of Actively Exploited Joomla JCE Flaw Allowing PHP Code Execution
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added a maximum-severity security flaw impacting Widget Factory Joomla Content Editor (JCE) to its Known Exploited Vulnerabi...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Fileless Phantom Stealer Targets Browser Credentials
item.description: In addition to executing entirely in memory, the malware's infection chain incorporates other anti-analysis techniques designed to frustrate detection....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Security Community Slams US Ban on Exporting Mythos, Fable
item.description: An open letter signed by dozens of security experts asked the government to reverse export restrictions on Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Malicious JetBrains Marketplace plugins steal AI API keys from developers
item.description: At least 15 malicious plugins found on the JetBrains Marketplace were designed to steal AI API keys from developers. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: SprySOCKS Windows Variant Abuses Kernel Drivers to Evade Detection
item.description: FishMonger, a China-nexus threat group, has deployed an undocumented version of the Linux backdoor against government targets in Honduras, Taiwan, Thailand, and Pakistan....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: New Rokarolla Android malware targets 217 banking, crypto apps
item.description: A new Android banking trojan named Rokarolla is targeting 217 banking and cryptocurrency applications using an extensive set of 137 commands. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Google Vertex AI SDK Flaw Let Attackers Hijack Model Uploads via Bucket Squatting
item.description: A flaw in the Google Cloud Vertex AI SDK for Python let an attacker with no access to a victim's project hijack the victim's machine learning model upload and run code inside Google's serving infrastr...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Steam Workshop abused to spread malware via Wallpaper Engine app
item.description: Threat actors are abusing Steam Workshop, Valve's community hub for downloading game-related content, to push various malware hidden in wallpaper packages. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ClickFix Campaigns Expand Malware Delivery With New Loaders and Fake Update Lures
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged multiple ClickFix campaigns that deliver three malware loaders called BabaDeda Loader, Lorem Ipsum Loader, and Potemkin, per independent reports from Morphisec, ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Rokarolla Android Trojan Levels Up to Full Device Control, Persistence
item.description: The emerging malware, spread via fake TikTok and Chrome downloads, demonstrates an evolution by combining banking fraud with extensive device surveillance and remote control....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: 'Lorem Ipsum' Malware Pivots to ClickFix Delivery
item.description: New analysis shows the campaign, which uses compromised WordPress sites, may be linked to the ransomware and data extortion group Vice Society....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: UK to require ID or face scan before you can make social media accounts
item.description: Opening a new social media account in the UK will soon mean proving you're over 16 with an ID upload or a facial age scan, under a government ban on under-16s taking effect in spring 2027. Security ex...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: GhostTree Attack Abused Recursive Windows Junctions to Hide Malware
item.description: GhostTree uses recursive NTFS junctions to generate vast numbers of valid Windows file paths. Varonis explains how the technique could cause Microsoft Defender folder scans to never complete, leaving ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: FTC warns of record $3.5 billion losses to imposter scams in 2025
item.description: The U.S. Federal Trade Commission (FTC) warned that Americans lost $3.5 billion to imposter scams in 2025, with reported losses nearly tripling since 2020. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New Rokarolla Android Malware Steals PINs, SMS Codes, and Crypto Wallet Funds
item.description:
Security researchers at Zimperium's zLabs have documented a new Android banking trojan, Rokarolla, that targets 217 banking and cryptocurrency apps and packs 137 remote commands.
Together, they give ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Survey: 94% of Incidents Involve Anonymized Infrastructure. Teams Are Still Reactive
item.description: Security teams have never had more IP data at their disposal. Every day, analysts ingest enrichment feeds, geolocation data, reputation scores, telemetry, and threat intelligence from a growing ecosys...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: CISA warns of another cPanel plugin flaw exploited in attacks
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has given U.S. government agencies three days to secure their servers against an actively exploited vulnerability (CVE-2026-54420) in t...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Attackers Exploit Three Fortinet FortiSandbox Flaws, One Patched Last Week
item.description:
Bad actors are exploiting multiple security vulnerabilities in Fortinet FortiSandbox, according to threat intelligence firm Defused Cyber.
In a post shared on X, the company said it has observed expl...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Ransomware gang abuses Microsoft Teams relays to hide malicious traffic
item.description: DragonForce ransomware used a custom malware named 'Backdoor.Turn' to hide command-and-control traffic inside Microsoft Teams relay infrastructure. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: China-Linked SprySOCKS Backdoor Expands to Windows with Driver-Based Stealth
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged two previously undocumented Windows variants of what was believed to be a Linux-only backdoor called SprySOCKS.
"The Windows variants discovered are internally ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Critical Fortinet FortiSandbox flaws now exploited in attacks
item.description: Attackers are now exploiting several critical vulnerabilities in Fortinet's FortiSandbox cyber threat detection platform, according to threat intelligence company Defused. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Windows version of SprySOCKS Linux malware used to attack govt orgs
item.description: Windows variants for the SprySOCKS Linux malware have been used in attacks targeting government organizations in at least four countries. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Fake Microsoft Alerts Used to Deploy North Korean NarwhalRAT Malware
item.description: The North Korean state-sponsored hacking group known as ScarCruft (aka APT37) has been observed using spear-phishing messages impersonating Microsoft Account security notifications to deliver malware ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: iRhythm discloses data breach, says hackers stole patient info
item.description: Digital healthcare company iRhythm Holdings has disclosed a data breach after hackers stole patients' personal and health information stored on third-party-hosted business applications. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Cisco Releases Security Updates for Actively Exploited SD-WAN Manager Flaw
item.description:
Cisco has released security updates for a medium-severity security flaw in Catalyst SD-WAN Manager that has come under active exploitation in the wild.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20262, c...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: CISA Flags LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin Flaw Exploited for Root Privilege Escalation
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added a security flaw impacting LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, requiring Federal Civ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: DOJ seizes CFAKE, SOCFAKE deepfake nude sites under TAKE IT DOWN Act
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice announced Friday that it has seized the CFAKE.com and SOCFAKE.com websites, which allegedly hosted nonconsensual AI-generated nude images and videos of women, in what ap...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
